title: hIndexOverYears
text: Compute visualize h-index trajectories Scopus exports. hIndexOverYears provides lightweight workflow exploring bibliometric trajectories, citation dynamics, h-index evolution time using citation data exported Scopus. R-package motivated reading scientist’s h-index change time?, Jeff Ollertons, Professor Biodiversity Department Environmental Geographical Sciences University Northampton. great post highly recommend .

title: hIndexOverYears
text: observe h-index changed career time compare h-index change others scientists’change estimate long takes average get 1 citation highly accessed papers (publication date present).

title: hIndexOverYears
text: V1.5 2022-07-26. Make linear regression model. V1.0 2018-09-02. Create plots using ggplot2. V0.1 2016-02-17. First release.

title: hIndexOverYears
text: package focuses three main tasks: cleaning citation data exported Scopus computing h-index trajectories time modeling visualizing citation dynamics workflow intentionally simple reproducible.

title: hIndexOverYears
text: start analysis, want make data tidy. good thing Scopus keeps format every citation overview, data cleaning can performed one easy step. function clean read clean data . Data preparation Now, dataset ready analysis.

title: hIndexOverYears
text: can also use h.plot function tidy data show h Index evolution years. starting year correspond h=0, can enter h value argument:  may also want use citation_speed function list highly cited papers (top10) get sense long takes get 1 citation. function shows default top10 cited papers, can give number papers argument. shows average time (months) per 1 cite.  Related , function predict_citations show number citations expected given interval time (example, 6 months) :  new functionality included v1.5 linear regression model. function fit_h_model job using arguments :  Finally, linear regression model can plotted using function plot_h_model takes arguments.

title: hIndexOverYears
text: package focuses simplicity reproducibility. Instead implementing large bibliometric frameworks, hIndexOverYears provides compact toolkit rapidly exploring academic citation trajectories using tidy workflows.

title: Estimate future citations over a time period — predict_citations
text: Citation projections based simple linear approximation: $$ expected\_citations = \left\lfloor \frac{term}{months\_per\_citation} \right\rfloor $$
